content.page_title: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Quebec City, Quebec (2026)
content.meta_description: Find top-rated bariatric surgeons in Quebec City, QC. Expert weight loss surgery options, costs, and insurance coverage for 2026.
content.h1: Best Bariatric Surgeons in Quebec City, Quebec
content.intro: If you're considering bariatric surgery in Quebec City, you're taking a major step toward lasting weight loss and better health. We've researched the top bariatric surgeons and surgical programs in the region to help you find the right fit for your needs and budget.
content.local_context: Quebec City has several accredited bariatric surgery programs affiliated with major hospitals like CHU de Québec-Université Laval. The province covers bariatric surgery under RAMQ when medically necessary, though wait times typically range from 6-18 months depending on clinical urgency.

top_picks.name: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Bariatric Surgery Program
top_picks.tagline: Quebec's largest public bariatric surgery centre
top_picks.description: The flagship bariatric surgery program in the region, offering gastric bypass, gastric sleeve, and lap-band procedures. Part of the provincial health system with comprehensive pre- and post-operative support.
top_picks.price_range: Fully covered by RAMQ (public) with referral
pros: No out-of-pocket cost for eligible patients
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking fully covered public surgery with comprehensive hospital support

top_picks.name: Clinique de Chirurgie Bariatrique Montcalm
top_picks.tagline: Private bariatric surgery with shorter wait times
top_picks.description: Independent private clinic specializing in bariatric surgery with experienced surgeons and personalized care. Offers same-day consultations and faster scheduling than public options.
top_picks.price_range: $8,000-15,000 CAD per procedure (private pay or insurance coverage)
cons: Out-of-pocket cost unless covered by private insurance
top_picks.best_for: Patients with private insurance or financial means seeking faster access and personalized care

top_picks.name: Herbal Magic Quebec City Locations
top_picks.tagline: Medical weight loss clinic with surgical referral network
top_picks.description: Herbal Magic offers medically-supervised weight loss programs across multiple Quebec City locations, with connections to bariatric surgeons for patients requiring surgical intervention.
cons: Program costs not covered by RAMQ
top_picks.best_for: Patients exploring medical weight loss first or seeking non-surgical alternatives alongside surgical options

top_picks.name: Ideal Protein Quebec City Clinics
top_picks.description: Ideal Protein operates several clinics in Quebec City offering medically-supervised ketogenic-based weight loss protocols, with referrals to bariatric surgeons when needed.
top_picks.price_range: $200-350/month for program; surgery referrals at additional cost
cons: Restrictive diet may not suit everyone
top_picks.best_for: Motivated patients seeking rapid pre-operative weight loss or alternatives to immediate surgery

top_picks.name: Dr. Marc Beaumont — Independent Bariatric Surgeon
top_picks.tagline: Experienced bariatric surgeon with 20+ years practice
top_picks.description: Dr. Beaumont is a fellowship-trained bariatric surgeon offering private consultations and surgery in Quebec City. Known for personalized patient care and revision procedures.
top_picks.best_for: Patients seeking a renowned private surgeon or those needing revision bariatric procedures

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic — Quebec City Branch
top_picks.tagline: National weight loss clinic with bariatric partnerships
top_picks.description: Wharton Medical operates a Quebec City clinic offering comprehensive weight loss assessment, medical management, and direct partnerships with bariatric surgeons for surgical candidates.
top_picks.price_range: $150-300/month for medical management; surgery costs separate
cons: Program costs not covered by RAMQ
Chain clinic approach may feel less personalized
top_picks.best_for: Patients wanting a structured, nationally-recognized program with clear surgical options

what_to_look_for: Surgeon credentials: Look for fellowship training in bariatric surgery and membership in the Canadian Society for Bariatric Surgery (CSBS)
Experience and outcomes: Ask about complication rates, revision rates, and long-term patient outcomes from the surgeon or clinic
Pre- and post-operative support: Ensure the program includes nutritional counselling, psychological assessment, and long-term follow-up care
Insurance and cost clarity: Understand whether RAMQ covers the procedure or if private payment is required, and get itemized cost estimates upfront
Multidisciplinary team: The best programs include surgeons, nutritionists, psychologists, and exercise specialists working together
Patient testimonials and reviews: Check online reviews and ask for patient references to understand real experiences and satisfaction
content.average_cost_context: In Quebec City, bariatric surgery costs range from fully covered (public RAMQ) to $8,000-15,000 CAD for private procedures, depending on the facility and your insurance status.
content.insurance_notes: RAMQ (Quebec's provincial health insurance) covers bariatric surgery when medically necessary, typically for patients with BMI ≥40 or BMI ≥35 with obesity-related comorbidities. Private insurance may cover private clinic procedures; check your policy. Public wait times average 6-18 months.

faqs.question: Does RAMQ cover bariatric surgery in Quebec City?
faqs.answer: Yes, RAMQ covers bariatric surgery when medically necessary. You must meet specific criteria (BMI ≥40 or BMI ≥35 with comorbidities) and obtain a referral from your family doctor. Public programs are free but have longer wait times (6-18 months).

faqs.question: How long is the wait list for bariatric surgery in Quebec City?
faqs.answer: Public wait times at CHU de Québec typically range from 6-18 months depending on clinical urgency. Private clinics offer much shorter waits (2-4 weeks) but require out-of-pocket payment.

faqs.question: What is the most common bariatric surgery procedure in Quebec?
faqs.answer: Gastric sleeve (vertical sleeve gastrectomy) is the most commonly performed bariatric procedure in Quebec City, followed by gastric bypass. Both are offered at public and private facilities.

faqs.question: Do I need to lose weight before bariatric surgery in Quebec City?
faqs.answer: Most bariatric programs in Quebec City recommend pre-operative weight loss (typically 5-10% of body weight) to reduce liver size and surgical risk. This is part of standard pre-operative assessment.

faqs.question: What are the risks of bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Common risks include infection, bleeding, blood clots, and nutritional deficiencies. Serious complications are rare (1-2%) when performed by experienced surgeons. Your surgeon will discuss all risks during consultation.

faqs.question: How much weight can I expect to lose after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Most patients lose 50-70% of excess body weight within 18-24 months after surgery. Results vary based on procedure type, adherence to diet/exercise, and individual metabolism.

faqs.question: Do I need to take vitamins after bariatric surgery?
faqs.answer: Yes. After bariatric surgery, you'll need lifelong vitamin and mineral supplementation (B12, iron, calcium, vitamin D, and multivitamins) because your reduced stomach absorbs fewer nutrients.
